english: to come Glr. — 2. sometimes to command, to order, complete form: bka (bka') gnáṅ-ba (gnang ba); gnáṅ-tsig (gnang tshig) skúl-ba (skul ba) to order a person to do a thing Pth. — 3. in complimentary phrases used in C. the precise meaning of gnáṅ-ba (gnang ba) is not always quite obvious: gnáṅ-rógs (gnang rogs) mdzad-pa (mdzad pa) (v. above) to give, to help to, to assist in(?); *gón-pa tsóm-pa ma naṅ* (*dgongs pa tshom pa ma gnang*), do not be put out, do not give way to any misgivings (towards me)! sometimes snaṅ (snang) (q.v.) would make a better sense. II. sbst. concession, permission, grant, gró-bai (gro ba'i) gnáṅ-ba (gnang ba) żú-ba (zhu ba) Mil.; mi-las (mi las) gnáṅ-ba (gnang ba) tób-pa (thob pa) to obtain permission from a person; bka-gnáṅ-ba (bka' gnang ba) (magisterial) permission, order (of government); gnáṅ-sbyín (gnang sbyin) very frq., gift, donation, present, stón-mo (ston mo) gnáṅ-sbyin (gnang sbyin) a present of provisions Glr.; gift of honour, reward, favour, privilege, price of victory held out etc.

adv. 1. on the third day, e.g. he came Glr.; gen. of the future: the day after to-morrow, saṅ (sang) gnaṅs (gnangs) Glr.; *tó-re náṅ-la* (*tho re gnangs la*) W. to-morrow and the day after to-morrow; saṅ (sang) gro (gro) gnaṅs (gnangs) gro (gro) yód-pa (yod pa) yin (yin) to-morrow or the day after to-morrow I must be off Pth.; gnaṅs-gzés (gnangs gzes) on the third and fourth day Lex. — 2. gnaṅs-čé (gnangs che) rather (too) large, gnaṅs-čúṅ (gnangs chung) rather (too) small Mil. nt.

1. the main point, object or substance, the pith, essence, gnad (gnad) gról-ba (grol ba) to explain the main point Mil.; gnad-dón (gnad don) the proper meaning, the pith of the matter Tar., Schf.; *gnád-ṡes-mkán* (*gnad shes mkhan*) W. one that knows a thing thoroughly, that is up to it, knows how to do it; *né' de'-pa, né'-kyi żú-wa búl-wa* (*ne' de' pa, ne' kyi zhu wa bul wa*) C. to excuse one's self, to defend or justify one's self (prop. to account for the circumstances that led to an action); *pog da pog; nad-du* (or *nad-daṅ*) *ma teb* (*phog da phog; nad du* [or *nad dang*] *ma theb*) W. I have hit (him), but not mortally; so B.: gnád-du (gnad du) snún-pa (snun pa) to pierce mortally. — 2. in anatomy: by gnad (gnad) bdun, or 'the seven important parts of the body', acc. to S.g. are meant: flesh, fat, bones and veins, and ču-rgyus (chu rgyus), don (don), and snod (snod) (Wise, Hindoo Medicine p. 69, gives a somewhat different explanation). — 3. in mysticism: the seven physical conditions requisite for successful meditation, lág-pa (lag pa) mnyam-bżág-tu (mnyam bzhag tu) bżág-pa (bzhag pa) (the hands joined over the stomach in such a manner, that the fore-joints of the fingers cover each other, whilst the thumbs are stretched out without touching), lus (lus) rdo-rje-skyil-krúṅ (rdo rje skyil krung) sdód-pa (sdod pa), gal-tsíg (gal tshig) mda (mda') ltar (ltar) srúṅ-ba (srung ba), dpúṅ-pa (dpung pa) rgód-ṡog-pa (rgod shog pa) ltar (ltar) srúṅ-ba (srung ba), mig (mig) sna-rtsér (sna rtser) o_bébs-pa ('bebs pa), mču (mchu) raṅ-o_bab-tu (rang 'bab tu) bżág-pa (bzhag pa), lcé-rtse (lce rtse) ya-dkáṅ-la (ya dkang la) sbyár-ba (sbyar ba); there are also séms-kyi (sems kyi) gnad (gnad) Mil. certain conditions of the mind required, such as abstaining from rtóg-pa (rtog pa), speculative thinking.

1. heaven, sky, = nám-mk'a (nam mkha'); gnám-ga (gnam ga) id. Cs.; gnám-gyi (gnam gyi) gó-la (go la) the sphere or globe of heaven Cs.(?); gnam gyúr-ba (gnam gyur ba) Mil., mentioned in connexion with an earthquake, and prob. corr. translated by Schr. with thunderstorm, tempest; *nam kar-kór* (*nam kar kor*) W. now the sky is cloudless, - now overcast (inst. of *dkar-o_kór* [*dkar 'khor*]?); gnám-sgo (gnam sgo) 1. Sch. the gate of heaven(?). 2. C. trapdoor. — gnam-lčágs (gnam lcags), gnam-lče (gnam lce) Cs. thunderbolt, lightning that has struck; gnam-stóṅ (gnam stong) the thirtieth day of the lunar month, the day of new moon Pth.; *nam-táṅ* (*nam thang*) W. serene sky, fine weather. — gnam-tel-dkár-po (gnam thel dkar po) Glr. 99 is said to be a deity of the Horpa or Mongols, as likewise sa-tel-nág-po (sa thel nag po), and bar-tel-krá-bo (bar thel khra bo). — gnám-mda (gnam mda') Pth. shooting an arrow straight up into the air. — gnám-rdo (gnam rdo) Cs. = gnam-lčágs (gnam lcags), Schr. hail. — gnam-zlúm (gnam zlum) vault of heaven Sch. — gnam-yás (gnam yas) Glr. 95 is said to be a n. p., the name of a building — gnam-rú (gnam ru), resp. for gżu (gzhu), bow (for shooting), Cs. rainbow. — gnám-sa (gnam sa) heaven and earth, gnám-sa (gnam sa) brdéb-pa (brdeb pa) tsam (tsam) so that heaven and earth were mixed Glr. — 2. v. nam (nam), faulty, incorrect.
Glr., Lt., rnab (rnab) Sg., Ld. *ná-po, fem. ná-mo* (*na po, fem. na mo*), an antelope, found in Ld., Sp., Kun., Nepal and other countries;
